event: Christ Overturning the Money Changers' Table
symbols: overturned tables — Christ's righteous anger against the commercialization of worship
Christ's authority — the Lord of the Temple purifying his Father's house
description: A modern interpretation showing Christ overturning the tables of the money changers in the Temple. The scene depicts a chaotic moment of righteous confrontation, rendered in Stanley Spencer's distinctive style with solid forms and everyday English settings transposed onto biblical narrative.
theological_description: The cleansing of the Temple (JHN.2.13-22; MAT.21.12-13) is Christ's most physically forceful act. 'He made a whip of cords and drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and oxen. And he poured out the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables' (JHN.2.15). His words echo Jeremiah: 'My house shall be called a house of prayer, but you make it a den of robbers' (MAT.21.13; JER.7.11). This act declares Christ's authority over the Temple and anticipates the new Temple of his body (JHN.2.19-21).

scripture_refs.reason: John's account of the Temple cleansing

_meta.title: Christ Overturning the Money Charger's Table
